architecture.summary: A compositional framework treating neural and symbolic systems as interchangeable black-box modules integrated into a single architecture. Each module exposes methods for forward reasoning (deduction), inverse reasoning (abduction for symbolic; induction for neural), enabling bidirectional information flow between subsystems without assumptions on their internal structure. The framework enables efficient training of the neural component by leveraging symbolic constraints and reasoning capabilities.

claims.statement: A symbolic module with any choice for syntax and semantics, as long as deduction and abduction methods are exposed, can be cleanly integrated with a neural module and facilitate the latter's efficient training
Empirical performance exceeds that of previous work
The framework treats neural and symbolic systems as black boxes to be integrated as modules into a single architecture, without making assumptions on their internal structure and semantics
